vulnerability.reference: Reliance on static benchmarks as proxies for true alignment enables reward hacking by evolved prompts.
vulnerability.generated: ML engineers rely on static leaderboard rankings as definitive proof of capability, lacking adversarial validation against reward hacking.

impact.reference: Inflated benchmark scores mislead stakeholders about actual model capabilities.
impact.generated: Adoption of inflated benchmark scores leads to brittle real-world model failures, undermining safety and misdirecting industry investments.

dose_response.reference: Reward hacking exploits proxy reward blind spots, so reliance on static benchmarks and over-trust in automated optimization lead ML researchers to be misled by inflated scores, causing wasted resources.
dose_response.generated: Reward hacking via proxy-blind prompts, through ML engineers comparing on leaderboards, triggers reliance on static rankings as proof, leading to inflated scores, brittle failures undermining safety and misdirecting investments.
